rent.note: furnished 1BR in nomad area; city center avg $1,178; broader furnished furnished market ~€1,100-1,400

utilities.note: electric + water + internet; avg €269/mo utility bills reported; internet ~€35

groceries.note: home cooking; Albert Heijn/Lidl; €200-300/mo reported range

diningOut.note: The Hague affordable vs Amsterdam; cheap meal ~€7-10, mid-range dinner ~€25 for two

transit.note: OV-chipkaart monthly ~€88; good tram network; connects to Amsterdam/Rotterdam

coworking.note: hot-desk monthly; smaller market than Amsterdam; expect €180-280/mo

misc.note: gym (~€30), toiletries, entertainment, beach visits (Scheveningen nearby), buffer

accomSeasonality._doc: 12 monthly multipliers for RENT ONLY (Jan..Dec). 1.0 = base. The Hague medium-term furnished lets are stable year-round.

evidence.quote: A comfortable monthly budget in The Hague is around $1,840, which includes city centre rent ($1,178/month), mid-range dining, transport ($88/month), and utilities
A 1-bedroom apartment in the city centre averages $1,178/month. Moving outside the centre saves approximately $325/month, bringing rent to $853
Utility bills average around €269 per month in The Hague
The average monthly cost of groceries is €200–€300 per person, including shopping at local markets and cheap grocery stores
